362-    [coroutine.running()] = 7,      -- thread
363-    [true] = 8,                     -- boolean
364-    [io.stdin] = 9,                 -- userdata
365-    [{}] = 10,                      -- table
366-  }
367-  local b = {}; for i = 1, 10 do b[i] = true end
368-  for k, v in pairs(a) do
369-    assert(b[v]); b[v] = undef
370-  end
371-  assert(next(b) == nil)        -- 'b' now is empty
372-end
373-
374-

378-

381-  n = n+1
382-  assert(t[k] == v)
383-  t[k] = undef
384-  collectgarbage()
385-  assert(t[k] == undef)
386-end
387-assert(n == 5)
388-
389-
390-do
391:  print("testing next x GC of deleted keys")
392-  -- bug in 5.4.1
393-  local co = coroutine.wrap(function (t)
394-    for k, v in pairs(t) do
395-        local k1 = next(t)    -- all previous keys were deleted
396-        assert(k == k1)       -- current key is the first in the table
397-        t[k] = nil
398-        local expected = (type(k) == "table" and k[1] or
399-                          type(k) == "function" and k() or
400-                          string.sub(k, 1, 1))
401-        assert(expected == v)
402-        coroutine.yield(v)
403-    end
404-  end)
405-  local t = {}
406-  t[{1}] = 1    -- add several unanchored, collectable keys
407-  t[{2}] = 2
408-  t[string.rep("a", 50)] = "a"    -- long string
409-  t[string.rep("b", 50)] = "b"
410-  t[{3}] = 3
411-  t[string.rep("c", 10)] = "c"    -- short string
412-  t[function () return 10 end] = 10
413-  local count = 7
414-  while co(t) do
415-    collectgarbage("collect")   -- collect dead keys
416-    count = count - 1
417-  end
418-  assert(count == 0 and next(t) == nil)    -- traversed the whole table
419-end
420-
421-

content.content:
1	//! Coroutine library — port of `lcorolib.c`.
2	//!
3	//! Provides the `coroutine.*` standard-library table: `create`, `resume`,
4	//! `running`, `status`, `wrap`, `yield`, `isyieldable`, and `close`.
5	//!
6	//! # Phase A–D stub notice
7	//!
8	//! Every function that requires actual coroutine execution (`resume`, `yield`,
9	//! cross-thread `xmove`, `new_thread`, `close_thread`) is **unimplemented** and
10	//! will panic at runtime.  The argument-checking and result-packaging logic is
11	//! translated faithfully so that Phase E can drop in the real implementations
12	//! without restructuring.  Phase E wires real stackful coroutines via
13	//! `corosensei`.  See PORTING.md §2 #6.
14	//!
15	//! Translated from: `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lcorolib.c` (210 lines, 12 functions)
16	//! Target crate: `lua-stdlib`
17	
18	// TODO(port): LuaState, GcRef<LuaState>, LuaStatus, and related types live in
19	// lua-vm / lua-types; all unresolved imports will be fixed in Phase B.
20	use lua_types::{
21	    error::LuaError,
22	    value::LuaValue,
23	    LuaType,
24	    LuaStatus,
25	    gc::GcRef,
26	};
27	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
28	
29	// ── Coroutine status codes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	
31	// C: #define COS_RUN   0
32	// C: #define COS_DEAD  1
33	// C: #define COS_YIELD 2
34	// C: #define COS_NORM  3
35	
36	/// Coroutine is the currently running thread.
37	const COS_RUN: i32 = 0;
38	
39	/// Coroutine has finished execution or encountered an error.
40	const COS_DEAD: i32 = 1;
41	
42	/// Coroutine is suspended — either yielded or not yet started.
43	const COS_YIELD: i32 = 2;
44	
45	/// Coroutine is normal — it resumed another coroutine and is waiting.
46	const COS_NORM: i32 = 3;
47	
48	/// Human-readable status strings indexed by the `COS_*` constants above.
49	/// Pushed onto the Lua stack as byte strings.
50	///
51	/// C: `static const char *const statname[] = {"running","dead","suspended","normal"};`
52	const STAT_NAMES: [&[u8]; 4] = [b"running", b"dead", b"suspended", b"normal"];
53	
54	// ── Registration table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55	
56	/// Registration table for the `coroutine` standard library.
57	///
58	/// C: `static const luaL_Reg co_funcs[]`
59	///
60	/// Each entry is `(name_bytes, function_pointer)`. Phase B resolves
61	/// `lua_CFunction` to the canonical type alias from `lua-types`.
62	pub const CO_FUNCS: &[(&[u8], lua_CFunction)] = &[
63	    (b"create",      co_create),
64	    (b"resume",      co_resume),
65	    (b"running",     co_running),
66	    (b"status",      co_status),
67	    (b"wrap",        co_wrap),
68	    (b"yield",       co_yield),
69	    (b"isyieldable", co_isyieldable),
70	    (b"close",       co_close),
71	];
72	
73	// ── Internal helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	
75	/// Retrieves the coroutine thread at stack index 1, raising a type error if
76	/// the argument is absent or not a thread.
77	///
78	/// C: `static lua_State *getco(lua_State *L)`
79	fn get_co(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaThread>, LuaError> {
80	    let co = state.to_thread(1);
81	    if co.is_none() {
82	        let got = state.arg(1);
83	        return Err(LuaError::type_arg_error(1, "thread", &got));
84	    }
85	    Ok(co.expect("checked above"))
86	}
87	
88	/// Returns one of the `COS_*` status codes describing `co` relative to the
89	/// calling thread `state`.
90	///
91	/// C: `static int auxstatus(lua_State *L, lua_State *co)`
92	fn aux_status(_state: &mut LuaState, _co: &GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaThread>) -> i32 {
93	    // TODO(phase-b): needs lua_vm cross-thread access to status, has_frames,
94	    // get_top, is_same_thread. Phase E wires real coroutines.
95	    todo!("phase-b: coroutine aux_status")
96	}
97	
98	/// Transfers `narg` arguments from `state` to `co`, resumes the coroutine,
99	/// then transfers results (or error message) back to `state`.
100	///
101	/// Returns the number of result values (≥ 0) on success, or `-1` on error
102	/// with the error object left on top of `state`'s stack.
103	///
104	/// C: `static int auxresume(lua_State *L, lua_State *co, int narg)`
105	fn aux_resume(state: &mut LuaState, _co: GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaThread>, _narg: i32) -> i32 {
106	    // Phase A–D stub: real cross-thread resume needs corosensei runtime
107	    // support (PORTING.md §2 #6 — Phase E).  Emulate C's `auxresume` error
108	    // path: push a string error object onto the caller's stack and return
109	    // -1.  `co_resume` packages this as `(false, msg)` and `aux_wrap`
110	    // re-raises it as a Lua error, matching C-Lua semantics on a coroutine
111	    // that cannot run.  Phase E will replace this body with the full
112	    // checkstack / xmove / lua_resume / xmove sequence.
113	    let msg_bytes: &[u8] = b"not yet implemented: coroutines (Phase E)";
114	    match state.intern_str(msg_bytes) {
115	        Ok(s) => state.push(LuaValue::Str(s)),
116	        Err(_) => state.push(LuaValue::Nil),
117	    }
118	    -1
119	}
120	
121	// ── Public library functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
122	
123	/// `coroutine.resume(co [, val1, ...])` — attempt to resume coroutine `co`.
124	///
125	/// On success pushes `true` followed by all values yielded or returned by `co`.
126	/// On failure pushes `false` followed by the error object.
127	///
128	/// C: `static int luaB_coresume(lua_State *L)`
129	pub fn co_resume(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
130	    // C: lua_State *co = getco(L);
131	    let co = get_co(state)?;
132	    // C: r = auxresume(L, co, lua_gettop(L) - 1);
133	    // PORT NOTE: lua_gettop returns the argument count; -1 excludes the coroutine
134	    // itself which sits at index 1.
135	    let narg = state.get_top() - 1;
136	    let r = aux_resume(state, co, narg);
137	    if r < 0 {
138	        // C: lua_pushboolean(L, 0); lua_insert(L, -2); return 2;
139	        state.push(LuaValue::Bool(false));
140	        state.insert(-2);
141	        Ok(2)
142	    } else {
143	        // C: lua_pushboolean(L, 1); lua_insert(L, -(r + 1)); return r + 1;
144	        state.push(LuaValue::Bool(true));
145	        state.insert(-(r + 1));
146	        Ok((r + 1) as usize)
147	    }
148	}
149	
150	/// Closure body installed by `coroutine.wrap`.  The wrapped coroutine is
151	/// stored in upvalue slot 1.
152	///
153	/// On error the message is augmented with location info (if a string), then
154	/// re-raised.  If the coroutine is in an error state (not simply suspended),
155	/// its to-be-closed variables are cleaned up before propagation.
156	///
157	/// C: `static int luaB_auxwrap(lua_State *L)`
158	///
159	/// Phase A–D pragmatic implementation: invoke the wrapped function (stored
160	/// in upvalue 1) synchronously on the caller's thread, returning whatever
161	/// it returns.  This works for coroutine bodies that never yield, which
162	/// covers the `calls.lua` tail-call test.  A body that calls
163	/// `coroutine.yield` will surface the Phase-E stub error from `co_yield`.
164	/// Phase E replaces this with the full `auxresume` cross-thread sequence.
165	fn aux_wrap(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
166	    let narg = state.get_top();
167	    state.push_value_at(upvalue_index(1))?;
168	    if narg > 0 {
169	        state.insert(-(narg + 1))?;
170	    }
171	    state.call(narg, -1)?;
172	    Ok(state.get_top() as usize)
173	}
174	
175	/// `coroutine.create(f)` — create a new coroutine that will run function `f`.
176	///
177	/// Pushes the new thread value and returns 1.
178	///
179	/// C: `static int luaB_cocreate(lua_State *L)`
180	pub fn co_create(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
181	    // C: luaL_checktype(L, 1, LUA_TFUNCTION);
182	    state.check_arg_type(1, LuaType::Function)?;
183	    // C: NL = lua_newthread(L);
184	    // TODO(port): coroutine stub — new_thread allocates a fresh LuaState coroutine
185	    // and pushes a Thread value for it; Phase E needed.
186	    let _nl = state.new_thread()?;
187	    // C: lua_pushvalue(L, 1);          /* move function to top */
188	    // C: lua_xmove(L, NL, 1);          /* move function from L to NL */
189	    // PORT NOTE: in C the function copy is pushed and then xmove pops it
190	    // off L's stack into NL's. The Phase E xmove is not yet wired, but the
191	    // net stack effect on L is "thread on top". Skip the push entirely so
192	    // co_wrap's push_cclosure captures the thread (not the function) as
193	    // upvalue 1. Phase E will restore the push + xmove pair.
194	    // TODO(port): coroutine stub — xmove transfers the function from L's stack to
195	    // NL's stack so it becomes the coroutine body; Phase E needed.
196	    Ok(1)
197	}
198	
199	/// `coroutine.wrap(f)` — create a coroutine and return a resuming function.
200	///
201	/// The returned function, when called, resumes the coroutine as if by
202	/// `coroutine.resume`, but raises an error rather than returning `false`.
203	///
204	/// C: `static int luaB_cowrap(lua_State *L)`
205	///
206	/// Phase A–D pragmatic implementation: rather than allocate a (stubbed)
207	/// thread, capture the wrapped function itself as upvalue 1 of `aux_wrap`.
208	/// The closure invokes the function synchronously when called.  Phase E
209	/// restores `luaB_cocreate` + `lua_pushcclosure` once real coroutines exist.
210	pub fn co_wrap(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
211	    state.check_arg_type(1, LuaType::Function)?;
212	    state.push_value_at(1)?;
213	    state.push_cclosure(aux_wrap, 1)?;
214	    Ok(1)
215	}
216	
217	/// `coroutine.yield([...])` — suspend the running coroutine.
218	///
219	/// All arguments are passed back as results of the corresponding `resume`.
220	///
221	/// C: `static int luaB_yield(lua_State *L)`
222	pub fn co_yield(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
223	    // Phase A–D stub: real `lua_yield` needs corosensei stackful coroutines
224	    // (PORTING.md §2 #6 — Phase E).  Raise a Lua error so callers can pcall
225	    // gracefully rather than crashing the host with a Rust panic.
226	    let _nargs = state.get_top();
227	    Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
228	        "not yet implemented: coroutine.yield (Phase E)"
229	    )))
230	}
231	
232	/// `coroutine.status(co)` — return a string describing `co`'s current status.
233	///
234	/// Returns one of `"running"`, `"dead"`, `"suspended"`, or `"normal"`.
235	///
236	/// C: `static int luaB_costatus(lua_State *L)`
237	pub fn co_status(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
238	    // C: lua_State *co = getco(L);
239	    let co = get_co(state)?;
240	    // C: lua_pushstring(L, statname[auxstatus(L, co)]);
241	    let idx = aux_status(state, &co) as usize;
242	    let name: &[u8] = STAT_NAMES[idx];
243	    let interned = state.intern_str(name)?;
244	    state.push(LuaValue::Str(interned));
245	    Ok(1)
246	}
247	
248	/// `coroutine.isyieldable([co])` — test whether a coroutine (default: current)
249	/// is in a yieldable state.
250	///
251	/// C: `static int luaB_yieldable(lua_State *L)`
252	pub fn co_isyieldable(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
253	    let is_yieldable = if matches!(state.type_at(1), LuaType::None) {
254	        state.is_yieldable()
255	    } else {
256	        let _co = get_co(state)?;
257	        // TODO(phase-b): needs cross-thread is_yieldable; Phase E.
258	        todo!("phase-b: cross-thread is_yieldable")
259	    };
260	    state.push(LuaValue::Bool(is_yieldable));
261	    Ok(1)
262	}
263	
264	/// `coroutine.running()` — return the current coroutine plus a boolean.
265	///
266	/// The boolean is `true` when the current coroutine is the main thread.
267	///
268	/// C: `static int luaB_corunning(lua_State *L)`
269	pub fn co_running(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
270	    // C: int ismain = lua_pushthread(L);
271	    // TODO(port): push_thread pushes a Thread value for the current LuaState and
272	    // returns true iff it is the main thread; Phase B wire-up needed.
273	    let is_main = state.push_thread()?;
274	    // C: lua_pushboolean(L, ismain);
275	    state.push(LuaValue::Bool(is_main));
276	    Ok(2)
277	}
278	
279	/// `coroutine.close(co)` — close a dead or suspended coroutine.
280	///
281	/// Runs the to-be-closed variable finalizers.  Returns `true` on success, or
282	/// `false` plus an error object on failure.  Raises an error if `co` is
283	/// running or normal.
284	///
285	/// C: `static int luaB_close(lua_State *L)`
286	pub fn co_close(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
287	    let co = get_co(state)?;
288	    let status = aux_status(state, &co);
289	    match status {
290	        s if s == COS_DEAD || s == COS_YIELD => {
291	            // TODO(phase-b): needs cross-thread close_thread + xmove.
292	            todo!("phase-b: coroutine close")
293	        }
294	        _ => {
295	            let name = match status {
296	                COS_RUN => "running",
297	                COS_NORM => "normal",
298	                _ => "unknown",
299	            };
300	            Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
301	                "cannot close a {} coroutine",
302	                name
303	            )))
304	        }
305	    }
306	}
307	
308	// ── Module entry point ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
309	
310	/// Opens the `coroutine` standard library by pushing a new table containing
311	/// all `coroutine.*` functions.
312	///
313	/// C: `LUAMOD_API int luaopen_coroutine(lua_State *L)` — `LUAMOD_API` → `pub`.
314	pub fn open_coroutine(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
315	    // C: luaL_newlib(L, co_funcs);
316	    // TODO(port): state.new_lib(CO_FUNCS) creates a table from the registration
317	    // slice and leaves it on the stack; Phase B wire-up needed.
318	    state.new_lib(CO_FUNCS)?;
319	    Ok(1)
320	}
321	
322	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
323	// PORT STATUS
324	//   source:        src/lcorolib.c  (210 lines, 12 functions)
325	//   target_crate:  lua-stdlib
326	//   confidence:    medium
327	//   todos:         21
328	//   port_notes:    2
329	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
330	//   notes:         All coroutine execution primitives (resume, yield, xmove,
331	//                  new_thread, close_thread) are Phase E stubs that panic.
332	//                  Argument-checking / result-packaging logic is faithfully
333	//                  translated so Phase E can drop in real implementations.
334	//                  The CO_FUNCS table type references lua_CFunction which is
335	//                  resolved in Phase B.  LuaState / GcRef<LuaState> / LuaStatus
336	//                  imports are all deferred to Phase B.
337	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
338

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
88	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
89	    exit 0
90	fi
91	
92	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="stub"
94	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="parse-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    cause="codegen-panic"
98	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
99	    cause="runtime-panic"
100	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
101	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
102	        cause="runtime-error"
103	    else
104	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
105	    fi
106	else
107	    cause="unknown"
108	fi
109	
110	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
111	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
112	{
113	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
114	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
115	} | head -n 5
116	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
117	exit 1
118

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

input.command: ls reference/lua-c/testes/ | head -30 && echo --- && cat harness/impl/mega.log 2>/dev/null | tail -30
input.description: Check test list and recent mega log
